entry.title: Is it possible to register the same project ID multiple times?
entry.content:
We would like to create multiple projects with the same project ID. Is this feasible?

Below are the business requirements:

My customer consists of two group companies: Company A and Company B.
Their respective roles are as follows:

  • Company A (parent company): Receives orders from clients and outsources manufacturing to its subsidiary, Company B.

  • Company B (subsidiary): Manufactures products based on requests from Company A.

In this setup, both Company A and Company B use the same project ID to maintain consistency across projects.

Since Company A and Company B are separate legal entities, they operate under different company codes and controlling areas.
Therefore, it is necessary to register separate projects within each entity.

Is there a way to fulfill the above requirements?
It would be ideal if we could define separate number ranges by company code or controlling area.

I would greatly appreciate expert guidance on this matter.

entry.title: Material Classification in Purchase Order Printout
entry.content:
Material Classification in Purchase Order Print                              

Step 1: Create a Material Type “Configurable Material” in OMS2

Step 2:  Maintain Characteristics using Tcode CT04 and class type 001 (Material Class)

Characteristics 1:

 

Characteristics 2:

Step 3:  Create a New Class and assign the characteristics.

Step 4: Material Master has to be extended to Classification view

Step 5: Create Configuration Profile using Tcode: CU41.

 

Org Areas P (Relevant for Printing) must be selected for Printouts.

  

Step 6: Create Purchase Order – ME21N

Result: Material Characteristics appearing in Purchase Order Print out –

 

Regards,

Sagnik.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Delta flow with aggregation
entry.content:
Hi, I wouldn't go the way of transformation flow at all. I would load the data into the inbound table, consume it via a view, on top of this view create a second with the aggregation. The second view would be persisted. 

I am not sure if you need CDC at all. You can load the BW ADSO via ODQ (replication flow), or create generic like extraction and load via data flow. The replication flow is preferred, but can be done only on ADSOs with change-log. 

Logically the system cannot aggregate on CDC as it needs to aggregate on the entire data set, not just the delta part. 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/sap-datasphere-bw-odp-delta-via-replication-flow/ba-p/13739830

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/sap-datasphere-delta-extraction/ba-p/13699391

Cheers, 

Martin

entry.title: Re: Configure a tile on SAP Build Work Zone to manage Decision artifacts
entry.content:
@alealeale ,

Before SBWZ do you see the application Studio Home in your subaccounts HTML5 repository?

Following are the required steps to get the application in HTML5 repository,

  1. Create new service key and make sure html-apps-repo" have additional host ID's which includes app host ID for Studio home as well. I see almost 10 app host ID's in my list.
  2. Update your standard destinations 'sap_process_automation_service' and ' 'sap_process_automation_service_user_access'' with these additional app host ID's.(Complete list from new service key)

You already removed 2 properties.

This should display the application in the HTML5 repository which can then be further added to work zone.

Hope this helps.

Thanks,

Smita Patil

Hi Christian. it can indeed be that the SAC, or Browser take the time, not DSP. 

first check the menu->Perf. Optimization - Analyze story Perf. Check scripts, widgets, ... DB Calls 

you can also check the SAC statistics / Admin content --> there you will see where the time gets lost if SAC or Browser. 

I have observed in heavy stories that only a fraction of time was used in DSP, rest was SAC and about 50% was Browser. 

Martin

entry.title: Re: 2LIS_02_ITM did not get the deleted item of PO in ME2...
entry.content:
Hi Charlie

in ECC or S/4 you will never have phisical deletion of documents but creation of new documents of reversals- (same amounts with  - sign). So the balance between the two items will be 0. (deletion)

In BW these reversals are flagged by 0recordmode = R . In fact you have in your DSO this typical scenario with a new document EBELN with sign - and recordmode R .

Hope it helps

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to register the same project ID multip...
entry.content:
AFAIK The project ID is a unique key, index PROJ~E External ID (user key). The answer is no.

  • Imagine the number of programs you or SAP  woild have to modify to identify the right project based on a field that's not always present on the screen, starting with the conversion exit
  • Consider using a prefix or mask in the project ID

entry.title: Enabling Fast Change for All fields in ME21N/ME22N
entry.content:
Introduction:

Recently, I received a simple but interesting requirement from our client:
They wanted to mass change the Issuing Storage Location (RESLO) in Stock Transport Orders  ME21N/ME22N transactions.

At first glance, this seemed like a quick win using SAP's Fast Change functionality. However, I soon realized that the standard SAP system does not support fast change for some fields, including RESLO.

Initial Investigation:

I initially thought of using a BAdI to default the issuing storage location automatically as per business logic. But before going that route, I wanted to understand why SAP does not allow Fast Change for certain fields, especially since RESLO is a part of structure MEPOITEM (Purchase Order Item).

Root Cause Analysis:

After a bit of debugging and research, I discovered that, SAP internally uses a data type called MEGUI_MASSCH_ALLOWED_FIELDS.

This structure defines which fields in the MEPOITEM structure can be used in the Fast Change functionality

When comparing MEPOITEM with MEGUI_MASSCH_ALLOWED_FIELDS, it's clear that many fields (including RESLO) are simply not included — which is exactly why Fast Change isn’t available for them by default.

Solution:

To enable Fast Change for a field like RESLO, follow these steps:

1. Enhance the structure MEGUI_MASSCH_ALLOWED_FIELDS by Appending the desired field from MEPOITEM (e.g., RESLO)

2. After making the change, execute the report in SE38 - BALVBUFDEL, this clears the ALV buffer so that the Fast Change UI picks up your added fields.

Reference:

SAP Note: 2124015 - Fast Change: fields that can/cannot be changed in ME21N and ME22N

Takeaway:

SAP's Fast Change is a powerful tool — but it’s limited to fields listed in MEGUI_MASSCH_ALLOWED_FIELDS. If your required field isn’t there, don’t jump directly to customer exit or BAdIs. Instead, first check this structure — you might only need to append one field and refresh the buffer to meet the business requirement.

Regards,

Kannan Chokkanathan

entry.title: Re: License Matrix Grow with SAP
entry.content:
Certainly! Here's a clear explanation regarding the license matrix in SAP S/4HANA and specifically how it applies to Purchase Requisitions, based on your questions.

SAP S/4HANA License Types Overview (Relevant for Access Control and Usage)

SAP S/4HANA typically offers several types of user licenses based on the level of access and activities performed. The main ones include:

  1. Self-Service User (also called ESS - Employee Self-Service)

    • For occasional users with limited access
    • Can perform basic tasks like creating Purchase Requisitions (PR), entering timesheets, travel requests, etc.
    •  YES – This license does allow the creation of Purchase Requisitions using predefined Fiori apps like F1643 (Create Purchase Requisition) or F2229.

  2. Core User (also called Functional/Operational User)

    • For more regular users involved in core business operations
    • Includes the capabilities of Self-Service, plus more complex tasks such as managing orders, reviewing approvals, processing invoices, etc.
    • YES – Can also create and manage Purchase Requisitions. This license includes broader access to purchasing and supply chain functionalities.

  3. Advanced User

    • For users who configure, analyze, and run business processes in depth
    • Includes capabilities for analytics, master data management, and advanced operations
    • YES – Naturally includes access to PR forms and much more.

🧾 Fiori App vs. SAP GUI – Does Licensing Differ?

  • Licensing is based on the user type and activities performed, not the UI technology.
  • Whether you use the SAP Fiori Launchpad (Web UI) or SAP GUI (classic screen) to create a Purchase Requisition, it does not change the licensing requirement.
  • For example:
    • A Self-Service User can use either Fiori or GUI (if available) to create a PR.
    • What matters is what you are doing, not how you are doing it.

entry.title: Determining Primary vs. Secondary Employment in SAP SuccessFactors with SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
In SAP SuccessFactors, managing employee employment records can involve multiple assignments. As a result, it's important to differentiate between primary and secondary contracts. In this blog, I'll walk you through what I consider the easiest methods to determine whether an employee’s employment is primary or secondary, using various SuccessFactors API entities.

 

Introduction

Why It's Important:

In many HR systems, employees can have multiple employment contracts. Identifying the primary contract is critical for processes such as payroll, benefits, and performance reviews. In SAP SuccessFactors, determining whether a contract is primary can be done in several ways, depending on the data model and integration requirements.

Overview:

There are various ways to determine the primary employment contract in SAP SuccessFactors. These methods range from querying OData API calls to utilizing specific fields or conditions based on the employee's data.

 

Understanding the Data Model in SAP SuccessFactors

EmpEmployment Entity:

This entity holds essential employment data for an employee, such as employment status, job title, and user ID. The EmpEmployment entity can often link to multiple secondary assignments (contracts).

SecondaryAssignments Entity:

This entity contains additional employment records beyond the primary one, allowing an employee to hold more than one contract. We often need to filter for the primary assignment within this entity.

PersonNav and PerPerson:

These fields help navigate the relationships between employees and their assignments, making it easy to access data for secondary assignments.

 

Example for Identifying the Primary Contract

Example Scenario:

Suppose you have an integration with SAP Integration Suite running every hour, which exports changed employee data and uploads it to an external payroll system. However, the payroll system should only process primary employment records.

Solution:

You can leverage the EmpEmployment and SecondaryAssignments entities to determine whether an employment record is primary. After filtering out secondary assignments, you can export only the primary contracts.

Steps to Identify Primary Employment Contract:

  1. Query EmpEmployment for Employee Data:
    Start by retrieving the employment data for an employee by querying the EmpEmployment entity. This data typically contains general employment information such as the employee’s ID and employment status.
  2. Check for Secondary Contracts:
    Query the SecondaryAssignments entity to retrieve all secondary assignments related to the employee. If the employee has a secondary contract, you need to exclude it from the export process. This can be done in Groovy script.
  3. Export Primary Contracts Only:
    After filtering out secondary assignments, you can create a file for the primary employment records, which are then ready for payroll processing.

Simplified Example to Get Data for an Employee with Multiple Employment Records:

In a real-world scenario, you would filter based on the lastModifiedDateTime field. Here’s an example query to retrieve data for a specific user with multiple employment records:

https://<datacenter>.sapsf.eu/odata/v2?$select=personIdExternal,userId,startDate,personNav/secondaryAssignmentsNav/allSfProcesses/SecondaryAssignments_effectiveStartDate,personNav/secondaryAssignmentsNav/allSfProcesses/SecondaryAssignments_externalCode,personNav/secondaryAssignmentsNav/allSfProcesses/externalCode,personNav/secondaryAssignmentsNav/allSfProcesses/usersSysId&$expand=personNav/secondaryAssignmentsNav/allSfProcesses,personNav/secondaryAssignmentsNav,personNav&$filter=userId eq '112665'

 

Response Example:

<EmpEmployment>
	<EmpEmployment>
		<personNav>
			<PerPerson>
				<secondaryAssignmentsNav>
					<SecondaryAssignments>
						<allSfProcesses>
							<SecondaryAssignmentsItem>
								<SecondaryAssignments_effectiveStartDate>2025-05-29T00:00:00.000</SecondaryAssignments_effectiveStartDate>
								<SecondaryAssignments_externalCode>103802</SecondaryAssignments_externalCode>
								<usersSysId>112665</usersSysId>
								<externalCode>f51d499a542e485b924352d8040a2fcf</externalCode>
							</SecondaryAssignmentsItem>
						</allSfProcesses>
					</SecondaryAssignments>
				</secondaryAssignmentsNav>
			</PerPerson>
		</personNav>
		<personIdExternal>103802</personIdExternal>
		<userId>112665</userId>
		<startDate>2025-05-29T00:00:00.000</startDate>
	</EmpEmployment>
</EmpEmployment>

 

 

 

Groovy Script to Identify Primary Employment

Here is a Groovy script to help determine whether the employee has a primary employment record based on the presence of secondary assignments.

The goal of the script is to determine whether an employment record is primary or secondary. This is done by comparing the userId from the EmpEmployment entity with the usersSysId from the secondaryAssignmentsNav entity. If the userId is not found in the secondaryAssignments, it means the employment record is a primary contract. If the userId is found in the secondaryAssignments, it is considered a secondary contract.

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;

def Message processData(Message message) {

  // Get the body of the incoming message as a Reader (XML data)

  def body = message.getBody(java.io.Reader);

  // Parse the XML body of the message using XmlSlurper

  def xml = new XmlSlurper().parse(body);

  // Loop through all the children elements of the XML (typically these would be multiple records)

  xml.children().each {
    xmlItem ->

      // Set paths for EmpEmployment and PerPerson using navigation properties

      def EmpEmployment = xmlItem;

    def PerPerson = EmpEmployment?.personNav?.PerPerson

    // Get the userId from the EmpEmployment record, or set it to an empty string if not present

    def userId = EmpEmployment ? EmpEmployment?.userId?.text() ? : "" : ""

    // Check for multiple secondary contracts by checking if 'usersSysId' exists

    def isPrimaryContract = true // Assume it is a primary contract initially

    def multipleContactUserIds = PerPerson?.secondaryAssignmentsNav?.
    '**'.findAll {
      node -> node.name() == 'usersSysId'
    }*.text();

    // If there are multiple secondary contracts, check if the current userId is part of the secondary assignments

    if (multipleContactUserIds.size() > 0) {

      if (multipleContactUserIds.contains(userId)) {

        isPrimaryContract = false // Set to false if userId is found in the secondary assignments

      }

    }

    // Print whether the contract is primary or not (for debugging or logging purposes)

    println isPrimaryContract

  }

  // Return the processed message

  return message;

}

 

Conclusion

By using the EmpEmployment and SecondaryAssignments entities, you can effectively determine whether an employee’s contract is primary or secondary. Filtering out secondary assignments allows you to focus on primary contracts, ensuring that only the relevant employment records are processed for payroll or other HR operations.

This method ensures that you handle employment records correctly, making payroll integration seamless and ensuring compliance with business rules. Let me know if you have any further questions or if you'd like additional examples!

entry.title: SAP Business Suite Webcast
entry.content:
Cерія вебкастів: SAP Business Suite для різних ролей у вашій організації

Чудова можливість поглибити знання про SAP Business Suite через призму різних бізнес-ролей! SAP запускає унікальну серію спеціалізованих вебкастів, кожен з яких розкриває потенціал платформи для конкретних управлінських позицій.

📅 Розклад заходів (червень-липень 2025)

Час проведення: 11:00 CEST (альтернативно: 12:00 SGT, 11:00 EDT)

Тиждень 1: Стратегічне управління та фінанси

  • 24 червня - CIO & CxO Track: "Stay agile and resilient in uncertain times with SAP Business Suite" Як залишатися гнучкими та стійкими в умовах невизначеності

  • 25 червня - CFO Track: "Finance-led ERP offerings in SAP Business Suite"
    ERP-рішення під керівництвом фінансової служби

  • 26 червня - COO Track: "Transform Supply Chain with SAP Business Suite" Трансформація ланцюга поставок

Тиждень 2: Закупівлі та HR

  • 8 липня - CPO Track: "Reimagine strategic and operation procurement with SAP Business Suite" Переосмислення стратегічних та операційних закупівель

  • 9 липня - CHRO Track: "Connecting People, data and processes with SAP Business Suite for HR" Об'єднання людей, даних та процесів в HR

🎯 Що вас чекає:

  • Персоналізований контент для кожної управлінської ролі
  • Живі Q&A сесії з експертами SAP
  • Практичні кейси та реальні сценарії впровадження
  • Інсайти щодо оптимізації бізнес-процесів через SAP Business Suite

💡 Чому це важливо:

У сучасному швидкозмінному бізнес-середовищі розуміння можливостей SAP Business Suite через призму вашої конкретної ролі може стати ключовим фактором успіху. Кожен вебкаст розкриває унікальні можливості платформи для вирішення специфічних викликів різних департаментів.

🔗 Реєструйтеся на вебкасти за посиланнями вище та поділіться своїм досвідом використання SAP Business Suite у коментарях - які виклики вашої ролі ви хотіли б вирішити за допомогою цих рішень?

 

#SAPUkraine30 #SAPCommunityKyiv #SAP

entry.title: SAP Commerce Cloud Continuous Influence - Results of 06/2025 Review Cycle
entry.content:
SAP Commerce Cloud has completed the fifth review of Improvement Requests since opening the SAP Continuous Influence sessions in Q4, 2022. To date, we have received 282 Improvement Requests and more than 1500 votes across all session. We highly appreciate the active participation from customers and partners who contribute. Your requests, detailed description of use cases, and votes gives us an unique opportunity to connect with you at scale and use your input to prioritise and scope deliverables.

We have evaluated the top voted requests and also some requests with lower votes that we frequently hear through other feedback channels, such as Customer Technology Advisory Boards (CTABs), customer user groups, customer co-innovation, partner feedback and interactions with colleagues in the field, to mention just a few.

Results from 06/2025

In this CIS Review Cycle we have updated 23 Improvement Requests, as follows: 

    • 6 have been delivered and 1 were already offered
    • 8 have been moved forward in the product planning process with 4 accepted and 4 planned long-term
    • 4 have been updated with for long-term consideration, meaning they have not (yet) made it into product planning
    • 4 have been updated with not planned

In the tables below you can find an overview of the delivered, already offered and accepted Improvement Requests:

Delivered

Session

IR Number

IR Title

Status

SAP Commerce Cloud Business Features290282Add on product level min/max order qtyDelivered in Q1/2025
SAP Commerce Cloud Automation337809Make apache2-igc deployment configurable to maintain more then 50 endpointsDelivered in Q1/2025
SAP Commerce Cloud Business Features335574Limited Forgotten password token column sizeDelivered in Q1/2025
SAP Commerce Cloud Pre-built Integrations326402Consider the store session language while sending the price request to the CPS Pricing ServiceDelivered in Q1/2025
SAP Commerce Cloud Automation314925Feature to lock/unlock deployment for specific EnvironmentDelivered in Q1/2025
SAP Commerce Cloud Automation314279Urgent need for monitoring notifications on POD restarts due to any infrastructural or memory issuesDelivered in Q1/2025

Already Offered

Session

IR Number

IR Title

Status

SAP Commerce Cloud Automation341202Dynatrace to monitor SAP UI5 Fiori webSolution already exists

Accepted

Session

IR Number

IR Title

Status

SAP Commerce Cloud Automation345546Zero-Downtime Deployments - Restart Behavior - Over-Provisioning instead of Under-ProvisioningPlanned for Q3/2025*
SAP Commerce Cloud Automation348288API for changing Capacity Units (Consumption credits)Planned for Q3/2025*
SAP Commerce Cloud Automation325810Add Scaling control capacity in SAP Commerce Cloud APIPlanned for Q3/2025*
SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Experience299607Improve Server Side Rendering Config, Usage & Documentation 

* SAP does not commit to deliver any planned functionality. Plans are all subject to change and may be changed by SAP at any time for any reason without notice.

Continuous Influence Sessions for SAP Commerce Cloud

In the table below you find a list with links to the four Continuous Influence Sessions for SAP Commerce Cloud as well as to the results blogs for the full overview of Improvement Requests which have been updated in this review cycle. 

Please see this blog post if you have not yet registered for and accessed the SAP Commerce Cloud Influence Sessions and would like to learn more.

Session Name & LinkDescriptionResults Blog (per session, w/ the same content)
SAP Commerce Cloud AutomationFor technical users operating SAP Commerce Cloud instanceshttps://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/blog/3656
SAP Commerce Cloud Developer ExperienceFor technical users customising SAP Commerce Cloudhttps://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/blog/3663
SAP Commerce Cloud Pre-built IntegrationsFor technical users integrating SAP Commerce Cloud with other SAP solutionshttps://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/blog/3665
SAP Commerce Cloud Business FeaturesFor business (and technical) users with requests related to business capabilities of SAP Commerce Cloud https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/blog/3664 

Past Result Blogs

For results from previous CIS Review Cycles for SAP Commerce Cloud Continuous Influence you can check out the following blog posts:

    • Results of 11/2024 Review Cycle
    • Results of 06/2024 Review Cycle
    • Results of 11/2023 Review Cycle
    • Results of 06/2023 Review Cycle

We look forward to hearing more from and from more of you in the future!

entry.title: Quick reference for Variable Pay Individual View and Enhanced Variable Pay Individual View
entry.content:
The "Variable Pay Individual View" in SAP SuccessFactors is a tool that allows employees to view their individual bonus plans and payouts. It provides a clear and concise overview of how their variable pay is calculated and what they can expect to receive.

It is a personalized bonus overview which displays the individual’s bonus plan, target bonus amount, and payout period. It shows plan-specific details such as business goals, individual goals, and weightings etc.

It provides transparency, communication, data analysis, enhanced user experience to employees.


Here is a quick guide to help you understand the prerequisites and the difference between the standard version and its advanced version, the Enhanced Variable Pay Individual View.

Prerequisites
1- Variable pay individual view should be enabled in your Succession Data Model.

 

2- Enable live profile visibility from the Variable Pay template.

Compensation Home – Settings - Display Live Profile View after form creation (shows manager input fields)

3- Role Based Permissions:

  • User - Employee Data - edit/view for the Variable Pay Individual View item.
  • User -Employee Views - edit/view permission for the specific section where the Variable Pay Individual View block is configured.
  • Enable view permission of “vpindividualview” standard element to Employee Self role.

  • Grant read based permission from the Variable Pay templates to the relevant fields to the Employee Field based group / Everyone.

Note - The standard element sometimes starts with question mark, and it will appear with the below naming  in the RBP role so can choose the same.

??user.vpIndividualView?? instead of vpindividualview
You can also check and correct the name if the standard element” vpindividualview” is already enabled in Manager Business Configuration.


4- This applies to all the roles e.g. HR manager, Manager, we need to enable all above permissions to the role and provide field based permission from variable pay template.

Once the above permissions are enabled you will see the Variable Pay Individual View link from people profile under Variable pay section:

Click on the link to view individual view.

Individual View for Total Compensation Plans:

You can now see the bonus calculation in the individual view for variable pay in the total compensation templates as well from 1H 2024 SuccessFactors Release.

You can access the individual view for Total Compensation templates by going to the Compensation Information block of the People Profile for an employee. The programs that the individual is eligible for are displayed including the Total Compensation ones if variable pay is included on the template and it's enabled to show the individual view.

Enhanced Variable Pay Individual view 
SuccessFactors also offers a more enhanced version of individual view for the employees in which employees get a simplified view of their bonus components on the redesigned page when you’ve Enhanced Individual Variable Pay View option enabled.

Actions for All Plans - Company Settings - Manage Company Settings – Under Variable pay section Enhanced Variable Pay Individual View

It shows a payout comparison between the actual and target in a graphical representation for each plan, which allows employees to consume their data easily.

Note – Enhanced Variable Pay Individual view does not support custom fields so in case of custom fields if you are expecting to be shown , you have to switch off the enhanced view and use the regular one.

Also , even after following the above guidelines, if still you are not able to access the Variable pay individual view or it is showing blank, check for interoperability code at the bottom of your variable pay template.
As per the implementation guide Interoperability isn't supported.
Remove the code, then duplicate the template and relaunch a worksheet and it will work (Attachment)

entry.title: SuccessFactors: Search for people in your organization based on custom ID
entry.content:
Introduction

SuccessFactors Home page offers a search bar which helps employees find other employees. You can use the search bar to find employees by first name, last name, person ID external, Username and other supported fields.

Organizations can adopt different People Search Strategies and configure the system to meet their business needs. You can enable fuzzy search, enable additional EC fields for search to auto-complete, or hide certain employments or content appearing in the search bar.

As you are aware, the field Person ID External is the unique identifier of a person within SuccessFactors. However, few customers have a requirement to identify a person with other kinds of IDs. E.g.: ‘Associate ID’.  

I recently had a customer requirement to enhance people search to include custom IDs. In this blog, we will deep dive into detailed requirement and the solution options.

Requirement

The ‘Associate ID’ is a unique field which identifies employees uniquely in the customers IT landscape. All the integrations, custom solutions, 3rd party products rely on ‘Associate ID’ to communicate and exchange data, and this information is also stored in SuccessFactors.

Requirement is to enable the search bar capability to enter ‘Associate ID’ and return the employee data as if a Personal ID, User ID had been entered. It has following benefits:

  1. For troubleshooting issues in the landscape, users can easily search employees by ‘Associate ID’
  2. Administrators, Data analysists and reporting teams will be able to co-relate the data effectively.
  3. When high volume of results returned by first name, last name search, ‘Associate ID’ is the only way to filter the employees.

Workaround

Enable Employee Central Name Fields for Autocomplete Search. Link to help documentation here. This allows users to search for employees by the lot of other alternative names. Repurpose one of the un-used fields (For E.g.: Formal Name or Middle Name Alt2) to store ‘Associate ID’. 

Configuration:

Admin Center -> Company and Logo settings -> Enable ‘Search People by Employee Central Name Fields’

Testing

In my demo system, i have re-purposed Personal Information -> formal-name field to store ‘Associate ID’

When you type 891891 in the Global Header, the employee is displayed

As you can imagine, without this capability, users would have to run reports to find associated Person ID, User ID of the users and then perform search. 

I hope you find this blog useful. If you have any feedback or questions, please feel free to use the comment section

entry.title: Blog It Forward - Marssel Vilaça
entry.content:
I was blogged forward by by @DanielMateo in this blog post. It’s always a pleasure to be back at the "Blog It Forward" challenge and be invited by a great promoter of knowledge sharing. I appreciate the invitation, Dani! 

I’ve shared my story here in my previous BIF years ago, but a lot has changed since then. Today, I’m still fully connected to the SAP ecosystem working remotely, leading projects, and training professionals, but with a lifestyle that’s much more balanced. I live in Recife, close to nature and, most importantly, close to what matters most: my wife and our two amazing teenagers. Working from home since 2020 has given me more focus and the space to live with greater purpose.

I go to the gym every day to lift weights. It’s been part of my routine for years. As I focused more on physical health, I also started paying closer attention to my spiritual life. 

Outside of SAP, what I love most is traveling with my wife and two kids. Our favorite destination is Porto de Galinhas. Every time we can, we go there. We wake up early, walk on the beach with our golden retriever Bolt, and enjoy a weekend barbecue.  

Fernando de Noronha 

When we don’t go to the beach, we drive to the countryside in Bonito/PE, is one of our favorite palece. Hiking, waterfalls, rappelling... and Bolt always comes with us. He loves swimming in rivers and lakes near to the many local waterfalls. After all that, he usually sleeps for over a full day to recover.

 One fun thing I’ve discovered recently is hiking with dogs. Yes, we go in groups, each person bringing their dog. It’s a fun mess ... a good one! Occasionally, Bolt gets into trouble with other dogs. He’s usually sweet, but not always 🙄.

About my activity in SAP world, I recently shared my story in the SAP Community Member Spotlight, I continue to share content on my social media channels (Youtube, Linkedin and Instagram), while leading SAP projects and actively supporting SAP Community events. My journey in the SAP Community goes beyond technology. It’s a place where I found purpose, friends, and amazing opportunities.

Thanks for reading this far. See you online, at the next SAP event, or maybe even on a trail, in nature, hiking with Bolt.

Now, answering Dani’s question:

With SAP's continuous push toward cloud-based solutions, how do you see the role of traditional SAP consultants evolving over the next 3–5 years?

The traditional SAP consultant must evolve and no longer just do system configurators. But to be business process designers. Those who understand integration, see the business value, and can explain it clearly will stand out. At the end of the day, it's all about turning complexity into real results for clients.

To continue the Blog It Forward, I nominate @RobertaFernande, @michellemachado and @SimoneAzevedo and leave them this question:

With S/4HANA, RISE with SAP, and BTP evolving rapidly, what do you think is essential for SAP clients to stay ahead in technology and digital transformation in the coming years?

entry.title: SAP BTP Global Account audit logs using SAP BTP Auditlog service, SAP Build Apps and SAP Ai Core
entry.content:
Let’s assume you are responsible for your company’s global account on the SAP BTP and would like to know what happens in the global account itself and in the subaccounts, right? I’m going to show you one of the practical and (in my opinion) giving some fun solutions.

Let’s start with the BTP basics. The platform gathers and stores crucial information about what happened in form of audit logs. These logs are being gathered and stored on two levels: global account level and subaccount level. Access to the audit logs on the subaccount level is simple – you can use the SAP BTP service called Audit Log Viewer. It is free of charge as per date of writing. But situation on the global account level is different – there is no way to run any service directly in the global account, so you can’t use Audit Log Viewer here. But your logs are still there – you just need to expose them via REST API and then access them – again via the REST API.

 

Exposing the global account logs.

Here we need one of the SAP BTP services, the SAP AuditLog service. Keep in mind that there are two versions of this service: free and paid. The free version is enough if standard log retention period of 90 days is enough. But if you want to increase the retention period over 90 days, you need the paid, premium version. You also need the premium version if you want to store logs generated by your own applications, running in the SAP BTP. More information here: Retention of Audit Data written from SAP BTP Applications and Services and here: Retention of Audit Data written from Customer’s BTP Applications

To run any SAP BTP service, first we need a subaccount for this service (or services). I strongly recommend creating one subaccount, dedicated just to the platform management. I would call it “technical” subaccount. This “technical” subaccount would serve purely for SAP BTP management and monitoring purposes – no business-related solutions should be placed here.

First thing to do after subaccount creation should be establishing trust with your IAS (if you use IAS, what I strongly recommend) and assigning “Subaccount Administrator” role collection to the appropriate people.

Then, on the global account level, assign the Auditlog Management Service, “central” plan to the “technical” subaccount. Please note it must be “central” plan and not any other.

Go to your “technical” subaccount and find the “Service Marketplace”. In the Service Marketplace, find the Auditlog service and under three dots select “Create”. Now it is important to create instance of this service with “central” plan – this is the only plan allowing access to the global account audit logs.

When service instance is created, click the arrow on the right-hand end of the instance line, find section called “Service Keys” and press “Create”. The only information you need to provide in this step is how you want to name the new service key – nothing more.

When service key is generated, you can access the Auditlog Management service via the REST API – right now click the tree dots on the right-hand end of the service key line and select “download”. This will give you the Auditlog service’s service key in form of “txt” file, but in fact the internal structure of this file is JSON, so if you change the file extension from “txt” to “json”, you will see the service key JSON-formatted in any tool supporting this format. You will need this file in a moment. But before you can access the logs, you need to assign correct privileges to you and/or anybody else who should have access to the audit logs of the global account. The Auditlog Management service is unique in the way that it doesn’t provide ready to use role collection – instead it provides just a role “Audilog_Auditor”. You must add this role to one of the existing role collections or create your own role collection, add this role and assign this new role collection to the appropriate people – or preferably, map them to the IAS groups of users.

Destination definition.

Regardless of whether you use a Pro-Code or No-Code approach, the SAP BTP Destination will be very helpful—especially for authentication. Therefore, we’ll begin by defining the SAP BTP Destination that points to the SAP Auditlog service. Go to the SAP BTP Cockpit, to the subaccount where your application development environment is running. If you have no access to this subaccount or have no “Subaccount Administrator” nor at least “Destination Administrator” rights for this subaccount, you need to find someone who has such privileges, and this parson must perform the next action: define SAP BTP destination to the Auditlog service.

Creating the destination is a standard and easy process – you just need to provide information found in the Auditlog service’s service key you already downloaded, like service address, authentication type, authentication address, client id and client credentials. But for the destination to work properly in SAP Build Apps, we need also to define some “Additional Properties”.

The data for SAP BTP Destination:

  • Name: “Central_Audit_Log_Service” – it is up to you how you name it, but the name should be meaningful, and you should be able to tell what the purpose of this destination is just based on its name even after a year or so.
  • Type: HTTP
  • Proxy Type: Internet
  • URL: open the downloaded service key file and find the “url:” line. Note: this is the very first “url” you can see in the file. Copy the full address with “http://”, but without the quotation marks. It should look like this:
    https://auditlog-management.xxxx.xxxx.xxxx.ondemand.com
  • Authentication: OAuth2ClientCredentials
  • Client ID: copy the content of the “clientid” line you can find in the “uaa” section of the service key – again without the quotation marks
  • Client Secret: copy the content of the “clientsecret” line you can find in the “uaa” section of the service key – again without the quotation marks
  • Token Service URL: here you have to combine the content of the “url” line you can find in the “uaa” section – again without the quotation marks – with the following: “/oauth/token”.
    Note 1: do NOT use the first “url” from the service key file here! In this field you must use the “url” you find INSIDE the “uaa” section.
    Note 2: the full Token Service address should look like this: https://xxxxx.xxxxxx.xxxx.xxxxxxx.com/oauth/token
  • Token Service URL Type: Dedicated

Additional Properties:

The absolute minimum is the following:

  • BuildApps.Enabled = true
  • BuildApps.ApiType = rest

On top, I would suggest adding the following, to be able to use the same destination in the future in other SAP Build tools and for other purposes, like automation, for instance:

  • sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled = true
  • sap.processautomation.enabled = true
  • MobileEnabled = true

Save the destination and check the connection – it should give response like this:

 

 

Building the application.

I recommend using SAP Build Apps for this purpose as it is the simples, fastest and most funny way to achieve our goal.

I assume here, that you already have access to SAP Build Apps or at least you know how to get it. If not, please ask your SAP Account Executive or SAP partner.

Create a new SAP Build Apps project as usual. Ensure that the SAP BTP Authentication is enabled. The application pages structure is up to you – for example you could put the list of log records immediately on the starting page or if you prefer, you can make starting page just for information, describing application purpose and put here a button to open a new page, the one actually showing the list of log records – you can do it your own way. Importantly, you should create a page that displays a list of records. You can use any existing “List” artefact, but I prefer to do it my own way, using a row, which “repeats” with output from the Auditlog service call.

 

Task 1: Getting access to the Auditlog data.

Thanks to the destination we prepared in the previous step, it will be easy task:

  1. Go to the “Integrations” tab
  2. In the “Integrations” section press the blue button “ADD INTEGRATION” – new page will open
  3. In the section “SAP Systems”, press the “BTP DESTINATIONS” button
  4. You should see the “REST API Endpoints” section and your SAP BTP destination to the Auditlog service you created during the preparation phase (“Central_Audit_Log_Sertvice” in my case) – select it
  5. Click “INSTALL INTEGRATION” in the top right corner of the screen
    This would be enough to send requests to the Auditlog service via the “HTTP Destination Request” function block and get answers, but you need a bit more: you need a data structure, identical with the structure of the answer, to repeat the list rows with.
  6. Press the “Add REST API Data Entity” button – an “Add REST API Data Entity” form will appear.
  7. Fill the form this way:
    1. Name: records
    2. Autogenerate configuration: selected
    3. Entity path within the API: /auditlog/v2/auditlogrecords
    4. Enable Capabilities: select only “list” – no other capability can be used in case of the Auditlog service
  8. Press the “Add” button
    After a few seconds you should see your “records” entity with auto-generated 11 fields. These fields were automatically read from the service thanks to the SAP BTP Destination you defined during the preparation phase.
  9. Select the “list” capability and press the “Run Test” button. If everything was done correctly so far, you should get in a few seconds the “Status: OK (200)” response above the black window and list of records inside the black window. If this is the case, you can now start shaping the UI of your app. Otherwise you need to check all the previous steps in search of mistake.

Now have a look at the list of records, visible in the black window. You can see here the structure of the answer from the Auditlog service. It is a list of objects. Object has 11 properties – the same as auto generated fields in the “records” entity. But there is a difference: the last field of the “records” entity named “message” is of “Text” type, while in the actual response it is an object with several properties. In case of a simple application, just showing logs, you should leave it as it is, but note that there is a room for further improvements of your app in the future. Leaving it as it is, you can display the “message” object as plain text, but with some work it is possible to get direct access to each property inside the “message” object in the API response. But this is outside of the scope of this article.

 

Task 2: The start page.

I recommend here creating three pages:

  • Star page
  • The log listing page
  • The record details page

Creating an app, you get one page immediately – it is your start page, so you just need to create next two pages and name them in a meaningful way, something like “List of records” and “Record details”.

So, we have:

  • “Home Page” – it is our start page
  • “List of records” – it is the page where we will see list of records and will select one of them for detailed view
  • “Record details” – it is the page where we will display all the record’s details and eventually will add interpretation done by the AI

 

For the time being, we will put just two things on the start page:

  • The “Title” component for short explanation of the app purpose
  • A “Button”, to open the records list page

We could show the list of records immediately on the start page, but making separate start page will allow us in the future to easily add new functions, without messing with the list of log records.

Put the text you want to display to app user at the start and the “Button”. Select the button and change its label to something like “Show records”. Open the logic canvas and put “Open page” flow function on it. Connect output of the “Component tap” with the input of the “Open page”. Select the “Open page” flow function and in the right-hand part of the screen, in “PROPRTIES” tab, select the “List of records” page to open (assuming you named so your page which will show the list of records).

Press “Save” in the top-right corner of the screen.

 

Task 3: Displaying the records.

The logic will work like this:

  1. Show the “Spinner”
  2. Get the records from the Auditlog service and put it in the data variable
  3. Hide the “Spinner”
  4. Show the records

 

Select the “List of records” page (or whatever you named your log list page).

Step 1:  The “Spinner”

First put the “Spinner” on the empty page, to make waiting for logs to appear a bit more pleasant.

We will need a variable to control visibility of the “Spinner”. As this variable matters only on this page, we will make it a “Page variable”.

  • Having the “List of records” page open, click the “Variables” tab at the very top of the screen.
  • Click “PAGE VARIABLES” on the left-hand side
  • Click “ADD PAGE VARIABLE” icon at the center of the screen
  • Select “From scratch”
  • Name the variable “PgVarSpinnerVisibility”
  • Change the “Variable value type” to “True/false”
  • Ensure that the “Initial value” is set to “False”
  • Save

Come back to the “User Interface” Tab at the top of the screen and select the “Spinner” you placed on the UI canvas and:

  • Change size to “large”
  • Open “ADVANCED PROPERTIES”
  • Click the square icon just below the “Visible” icon – dialog opens
  • Select “Data and Variables”
  • Select “Page variable”
  • Select the “PgVarSpinnerVisibility” variable you defined a minute ago
  • Save in the dialog
  • Switch to the “LAYOUT” tab
  • Open “POSITION” and select the icon to align it horizontally to the middle
  • Save in the top-right corner of screen

 

Step 2: The data to display

Then we need to define a data variable, based on the “records” entity we have in the integration. To do so, still having the “List of records” page open:

  • click on the “Variables” tab at the top of the screen
  • click “DATA VARIABLES” on the left side of the screen
  • click “ADD DATA VARIABLE” next to the plus in the circle sign
  • select “records”
  • change the default variable name in the “Data variable name” field of the right-side properties section to something meaningful, like “DataVarListOfRecords”
  • ensure that the “Data variable type” is set to “Collection of data records”
  • having selected the “DataVarListOfRecords”, open the “Logic canvas”
  • cut the link between “Page mounted” event and the “Get record collection” flow function
  • Insert here the “Set page variable” flow function – connect its input to the exit of the “Page mounted” event and connect its exit to the input of the “Get record collection” flow function.
  • Select the “Set page variable” flow function
  • Select the “PgVarSpinnerVisibility” under the “Variable name” label
  • Select “True” under the “Assigned value” label
  • Add second “Set page variable” flow function
  • Connect its input to the exit of the “Set data variable” output
  • Select the second “Set page variable” flow function
  • Select the “PgVarSpinnerVisibility” under the “Variable name” label
  • Select “False” under the “Assigned value” label
  • Save

The function flow should look like this:

 

 

 

Step 3: Define visual structure of the list of records.

You can use here any ready to use list, available in the “MARKETPLACE” section, but I prefer to shape the list using “Row” as this approach gives me the full control over what and how is displayed on the screen.

We will show here only three fields, but you can modify this structure any way you want.

  • Put the “Row” component on the UI canvas
  • Change layout of the “Row” to have three cells (do it in the right-hand section, “LAYOUT” tab)
  • Define width of each cell as 1

 

  • Put “Text” component in each cell of the row

In the future you can improve the look of this page by adding borders, backgrounds, headers etc, but now we are going just to display the list of records the simplest way.

Select the row. Note you must select entire row and not just e cell. The easiest way is to use the “TREE” view at bottom-right part of the screen.

 

 

With row selected, go to the “PROPERTIES” tab in the section above the tree view and click the square icon labeled “Repeat with”.

 

A dialog will open – select “Data and Variables”, then “Data variable” and then the data variable we defined just a few minutes ago (“DataVarListOfRecords”).

Click “Save” (in the dialog) and then again “Save” (top-right corner of screen).

We have “Text” component in each cell, so now we will connect each “Text” with proper data to display.

Select the first “Text” in the “Row” and in the right-hand “PROPERTIES” tab click the icon just below the “Content” label. Dialog opens – select “Data item in repeat”.

 

Ten select “current”. Find and select “time” and press “Save”.

Select the second “Text” in the “Row” and configure it the same way as the first one, but this time select “category”.

Select the third “Text” in the “Row” and configure it the same way as the first one, but this time select “tenant”.

Finally, press the “Save” button in the top-right corner of the screen to save the application.

The last thing we will add on this page is a “Button” to come back to the start page:

  • Place a “Button” just below the “Row” on the UI canvas
  • Change label of the button to “Back”
  • Format the button as you like using the “STYLE” and “LAYOUT” tabs on the right-hand side of the screen
  • Click the “arrow up” icon at the very bottom of the screen, named “Add logic to LIST OF RECORDS”. The text “LIST OF RECORDS” is the name of this page, so if you named the page differently, you would see your name of the page instead of the “LIST OF RECORDS”
  • Find “Navigate back” flow function in the “CORE” tab of the “LOGIC CANVAS” and place it next to the “EVENT Component tap” block on the logic canvas
  • Connect exit of the “Component tap” with the input of the “Navigate back” flow function
  • Save

 

 

Step 4: adding full view of the selected record

Now we will take care of the “Record details” page of our app. The Auditlog service does not allow for requesting just one record, based on its id (at the date of writing), so we will do all the data preparation work on the “List of records” page we already have running and then write the record data to the Application Variable to make it available to the “Record details” page, which in turn will be responsible for displaying the already prepared data.

The procedure will look like this:

  1. User clicks on the desired record in the displayed list
  2. App logic of the “List of records” page searches for the desired record in the data variable “DataVarListOfRecords”
  3. App logic of the “List of records” page writes the data of the record it found to the Application Variable
  4. App logic of the “List of records” page opens the “Record details” page
  5. The “Record details” page shows record details

Create the Application Variables

We will need two application variables.

  1. Go to the “Variables” tab
  2. Select “APP VARIABLES”
  3. Select “ADD APP VARIABLE”
  4. Select “From existing schema”
  5. Select “Entity”
  6. Select the entity we created in chapter “B. Application building.” paragraph “Preparation of the data structure for the list of records to repeat with”. We named this entity “records”
  7. Change name of the app variable to “AppVarSingleRecord”
  8. Select “ADD APP VARIABLE”
  9. Select “From scratch”
  10. Change name of the app variable to “AppVarMessageText”
  11. Make sure the “Variable value type” is “Text”
  12. Save

 

Activate the row, so clicking on any record activates the process of displaying its content

  1. Open the “User Interface” tab at the top of the screen
  2. Open the “List of records” page
  3. Select the “Row” – ensure you selected the row and not a single cell nor single text. Use the “TREE” view in the bottom-right corner of the screen for precise selection
  4. With “Row” selected, open the “LOGIC CANVAS”
  5. Drag to the logic canvas two “Set app variable” and one “Open page” flow functions.
  6. Connect all the flow function in one flow: “Component tap” -> “Set app variable” -> “Set app variable” -> “Open page”.
  7. First “Set app variable”:
    • “Variable name”: select “AppVarSingleRecord”
    • “Assigne value”:
      • click the square icon – dialog will open
      • select “Formula"
      • click on the formula line
      • delete the current formula
      • type “find_by” – two suggestions will show – double click the “FIND_BY_KEY”
      • highlight the “list” in the formula
      • select the “Data variables” on the left
      • double click the “DataVarListOfRecords”
      • highlight the “key” in the formula
      • type here “message_uuid” – this time type it WITH the quotation marks
      • highlight the “value” in the formula
      • select the “Currently repeated data item” on the left
      • double click the “repeated.current.message_uuid”
        The final formula should look like this:
        FIND_BY_KEY(data.DataVarListOfRecords, "message_uuid", repeated.current.message_uuid)
        if you name everything exactly like in my example, you can simply copy this formula and paste it in the formula field.
      • Save
  8. Second “Set app variable”:
    We want to show the “message” written in the log record, but its format makes it hard to read. We will make small but meaningful changes in the message format and this is the purpose of this flow function.
    1. “Variable name”: select “AppVarMessageText”
    2. “Assigne value”:
      • click the square icon – dialog will open
      • select “Formula”
      • click on the formula line
      • delete the current formula
      • put
        REPLACE_ALL(REPLACE_ALL(LEFT_STRIP(RIGHT_STRIP(appVars.AppVarSingleRecord.message, 1), 1),",","\n"),"\\","")
        as the formula – it removes the opening and closing brackets, relaces comas with new lines and removes backslashes
      • Save
  9. “Open page”: select “Record details” page to open
  10. Save

 

Build the UI of the “Record details” page

We already have the “Record details” page, but we need to put all the fields on it and connect them to the correct data sources. If we were to show just data entity, defined in the “Integrations” tab, we could use the AI for this task (assuming AI is enabled in your SAP Build environment). But we will not show the data entity – instead we will show content of the two application variables: “AppVarSingleRecord” and “AppVarMessageText”. So we need to design the page layout manually.

  1. Get familiar with structure of the AppVarSingleRecord application variable – note the data fields
  2. Open the “Record details” page
  3. Put a “Text” component on the UI canvas for each field of the variable
  4. For each “Text” component EXCEPT the one for “message” field do the following:
    1. select the “Text” component
    2. press the square icon just below the “Content” label in the “PROPERTIES” panel on the right-hand side – a dialog will open
    3. select “Data and variables”
    4. select “App variable”
    5. select “AppVarSingleRecord”
    6. select the data field you want to display using this “Text” component
    7. Save
  5. Select the “Text” component in which you want to display the message
    1. press the square icon just below the “Content” label in the “PROPERTIES” panel on the right-hand side – a dialog will open
    2. select “Data and variables”
    3. select “App variable”
    4. select “AppVarMessageText”
    5. Save
  6. Put a “Button” component at the end of the page and select it
  7. Open the logic canvas and drag here the “Navigate back” flow function and connect it to the “Component tap” event
  8. Save the application

Now you can use the “Preview” option in the top-right corner of the screen to test your application.

If the application works, now you can spend some time beautifying the application by:

  • Adding header row above the list of records in the “List of records” page
  • Adding additional, static texts as labels for dynamic text fields – here you can use rows for structured view
  • Adding containers and placing fields in the containers – to add visual structure to the pages
  • Manage borders, paddings and margins of the components
  • Manage color scheme

Finally, when you are happy with your app, go to the “Publish section, create build configuration, build the application and deploy.

 

Adding the date filters

As the application already works, we can see, that sometimes there is a lot of records to display. The Auditlog service’s API is limited: it can (as per date of writing) send back up to 500 records. You will find that quite often you would like the ability to define date range of log records to display.

Variables to store dates

We need to create in total four application variables to store the selected dates and to process them the way we need. Let’s create them right now:

Go to Variables -> APPLICATION VARIABLES and define the following variables:

  1. “DateFrom”
    • Variable value type: Date/time text (ISO8601)
  2. “DateTo”
    • Variable value type: Date/time text (ISO8601)
  3.  “DateFromTxt”
    • Variable value type: Text
    • Initial value: 0000-00-00T00:00:00
  4.  “DateToTxt”
    • Variable value type: Text
    • Initial value: 0000-00-00T00:00:00

 

UI modification

For simplicity, we will modify the “Home page” – our start page. We will add there two components: one for start date and the other one for end date. We will use ready to use “Date field”:

  1. Open the “Home page”
  2. Press “MARKETPLACE” in the UI components panel
  3. Search for “Date field” – at least three options will appear
  4. Select the option named just “Date field”
  5. Click “Install”
  6. Exit the “MARKETPLACE”
  7. Select “INSTALLED” in the UI components panel
  8. Find just installed “Date field” and drag it twice to the UI canvas – we need two of them
  9. Place both just above the already existing “Button”
  10. Select the topmost “Date field”
  11. Choose the “DateFrom” application variable in the “Selected value” property
  12. Type “Start date:” in the “Label” field
  13. Switch to the “LAYOUT” tab
  14. Expand the “WIDTH AND HEIGHT” section and select “Fit content”
  15. Scroll down to “POSITION” and select the icon to justify horizontally to the middle
  16. Scroll down to the “Z-index” and set it to “101”
  17. Select the lower “Date field”
  18. Choose the “DateTo” application variable in the “Selected value” property
  19. Type “End date:” in the “Label” field
  20. Switch to the “LAYOUT” tab
  21. Expand the “WIDTH AND HEIGHT” section and select “Fit content”
  22. Scroll down to “POSITION” and select the icon to justify horizontally to the middle
  23. Scroll down to the “Z-index” and set it to “100”

Now we will set the default values for both fields. Let’s assume that we want to see the current day’s log records by default.

  1. Click somewhere in the empty space of the “Home page” UI canvas
  2. Open the logic canvas
  3. Find “IF condition” flow function in the “CORE” section of the logic components and drag two instances to the logic canvas
  4. Find “Set app variable” and again drag two instances
  5. Connect inputs of both “IF condition” flow functions to the “Page mounted” event
  6. Connect exit of first “IF condition” flow function to the input of the first “Set app variable” flow function
  7. Connect exit of the second “IF condition” flow function to the input of the second “Set app variable” flow function
  8. Select first “IF condition” flow function
  9. Select “Formula” in the “Condition” field
  10. Put
    appVars.DateFromTxt=="0000-00-00T00:00:00"
    in the formula field
  11. Select second “IF condition” flow function
  12. Select “Formula” in the “Condition” field
  13. Put
    appVars.DateToTxt=="0000-00-00T00:00:00"
    in the formula field
  14. Select first “Set app variable” flow function
  15. Set “Variable name” to the application variable “DateFrom”
  16. Select “Formula” in the “Assigned value” field and put
    NOW()
    in the formula field
  17. Select second “Set app variable” flow function
  18. Set “Variable name” to the application variable “DateTo”
  19. Select “Formula” in the “Assigned value” field and put
    NOW()
    in the formula field – yes, we need the same value here
  20. Save the application

 

Data entity modification

We need to modify the data entity we defined in the “Integrations” tab, so let’s go there.

  1. Open the “Integrations” tab
  2. Select the “records” entity
  3. Scroll down to the very end of entity definition
  4. Find “Additional inputs”
  5. Add two additional inputs:
  6. “date_from” of type “Text”
  7. “date_to” of type “Text”

     



  8. Scroll up to the “Relative path and query” field
  9. Click it and replace the formula with:
    "/auditlog/v2/auditlogrecords?time_from="+query.additionalInputs.date_from+"&time_to="+query.additionalInputs.date_to
    this way we simply add two parameters to the REST API call and we do it the way that both parameters are set dynamically at the call based on the additional inputs we defined at the very bottom of the entity definition

     



  10. Note that now we see both additional inputs in the “Test” section of the “records” entity. If you want to test the entity now, you have to provide both inputs in the form: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS. For example 2025-06-13T00:00:00 for “date_from” and 2025-06-13T23:59:59 for “date_to”.

     



  11. Save the application
  12. Go to the “User interface” tab and open the “List of records” page
  13. Go to the “Variables” tab and select the “DATA VARIABLES” section
  14. Select the “DataVarListOfRecords” – now you see the two additional inputs
  15. Connect the “date_from” input to the “DateFromTxt” application variable
  16. Connect the “date_to” input to the “DateToTxt” application variable



17. Save the application

“Home page” button function modification

The Auditlog service API expects date/time to be sent in exact format and does not tolerate any differences, so we must transform the Date/time value we receive from the “Date field” components to exactly the format expected by the API.

  1. Open the “User Interface” tab
  2. Open the “Home page”
  3. Select the button
  4. Open the logic canvas
  5. Cut the connection between the “Component tap” event and the “Open page” flow function
  6. Drag two instances of the “Set app variable” function flow to the logic canvas
  7. Connect everything in single flow, with “Open page” flow function at the very end of the flow
  8. Select first “Set app variable” function flow
  9. Select “DateFromTxt” in the “Variable Name”
  10. Select “Formula” in the “Assigned value” field
  11. Put
    SUBSTRING(appVars.DateFrom, 0, 11)+"00:00:00"
    in the formula field - we simply cut off everything what is after “T” in the “YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS” format. We need to do so as the format we receive from the “Date field” can vary and it contains milliseconds and can contain additional signs. Then we add the fixed time. In the future we could potentially make the time an additional, dynamic factor in the filters, though.
  12. Select second “Set app variable” function flow
  13. Select “DateToTxt” in the “Variable Name”
  14. Select “Formula” in the “Assigned value” field
  15. Put
    SUBSTRING(appVars.DateTo, 0, 11)+"23:59:59"
    in the formula field
  16. Save the application
  17. Test the application in the “Preview”
  18. If everything works, build and deploy

 

 

Adding Artificial Intelligence for log message interpretation

Messages included in the logs can be difficult to read, understand and interpret. Adding AI might help a bit.

 

Setting up the AI in the SAP AI Core

Here I assume you already have access to the SAP AI Core and either have privileges needed to setup the AI deployment or you already have access to the AI deployment you can use in the application.

In short, when you start with the SAP AI Core:

  1. You have to create a resource group, where your resources, like configurations and deployments, will be stored.
  2.  You need to create a configuration of the LLM you will use.
  3. Create a deployment of your AI LLM, based on the configuration from point 2.

 

Creating the SAP BTP Destination

To proceed further we need the following information:

  1. SAP AI Launchpad: copy the name of the resource group you use – my resource group is named “default”
  2. SAP AI Launchpad: open the deployment you will use and copy its URL:

 

 

  1. SAP BTP Cockpit: you need the service key of the SAP AI Core service – more precisely you need authentication url, client id and client secret.

After gathering all the information, we need to define the destination in the SAP BTP subaccount, where SAP Build is deployed, pointing to the SAP AI Core’s AI deployment we will use.

Name: it is up to you. I use ChatGPT4o, so in my case the destination name is AI_Core_GPT4o

Type: HTTP

Proxy Type: Internet

URL: this is the url of the AI deployment, you copied this url in the SAP AI Launchpad

Authentication: OAuth2ClientCredentials

Client ID: copy from the SAP AI Core service’s service key

Client Secret: copy from the SAP AI Core service’s service key

Token service URL: this is the “url” value from the service key + /oauth/token – it should look like:

https://xxxxx.xxxxxxxx.xxxxxx.xxxxxxx.com/oauth/token

 

Token Service URL Type: Dedicated

 

 

And some additional properties:

The minimum:

  • BuildApps.Enabled = true
  • BuildApps.ApiType = rest
  • URL.headers.AI-Resource-Group = name of your resource group in the SAP AI Core – in my case it is “default”
    Note here that this is helpful if you use just one instance of the AI, located in one resource group. But if you use more AI instances and these instances are scattered among different groups, you can’t put the resource group name in the destination – instead you have to manage it in the application, in the REST API call.

 

And additional for the future use:

  • sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled = true
  • sap.processautomation.enabled = true
  • MobileEnabled = true

 

 

Accessing AI from SAP Build Apps

Let’s go now to the SAP Build Apps.

  1. Open the “Integrations” tab
  2. Click “ADD INTEGRATION”
  3. Find the “SAP Systems” tile and click “BTP DESTINATIONS”
  4. Find and select the destination we just defined
  5. Click the “INSTALL INTEGRATION”
    That’s all we need to do on the integration level – we don’t need any entity here as we will just call the BTP destination and directly process the reply.

 

Adding AI-related components to the application UI

Open the “Record Details” page. Drag the following on the UI canvas:

  • A button, to send request to the AI – name it “Send request to AI”
  • A spinner, to show that the process is running
  • A text field with the AI’s interpretation

I would also suggest adding text fields to display cost of the interpretation denominated in the SAP AI Core tokens:

  • Input tokens
  • Output tokens
  • Total tokens

Now we need to define some page variables. Most of them we will populate with data we get from the AI:

  • PgVarInputTokens - number
  • PgVarOutputTokens - number
  • PgVarTotalTokens - number
  • PgVarAiInterpretation - text

But one page variable will be used to store the template of the request:

  • PgVarRequestTemplate – text – set the “Initial value” to:
    The following text is audit log record. Provide short interpretation in less than 50 words.

Note: We use the simplest possible approach in this demo app, suitable for very small, probably single person development team. But in the real life, production version it would make sense to use SAP AI Core prompt templating function. Especially when there is a dedicated AI team in your organization – in such case the application development team should use templates prepared in advance by the AI team for all the AI requests.

 

Another variable will be used to build request to the AI:

  • In correct form
  • Containing correct request

 

  • PgVarMessageToAI – this variable is an object, containing list of objects with two properties:

{

    "PgVarMessageToAI": {

        "messages": [

            {

                "role": "text",

                "content": "text"

            }

        ]

    }

}

 

 

 

Add also one variable to control visibility of the spinner:

  • PgVarSpinnerVisible – true/false – set the “Initial value” to false

Now we need to connect page variables with “Text” components: go to “User Interface” tab, open the “Record Details” page and assign each “Text” component its variable in the “Content” field.

The button logic

Now let’s build the logic to send request and to process the response. Select the “Send request to AI” button and open its logic canvas. Place and connect flow functions as shown on the following image:

 

 

 

Flow functions configuration:

  1. Set page variable PgVarSpinnerVisible to true
  2. Set page variable PgVarMessageToAI, field messages, to list of values:
    1. Role set to user
    2. Content set to formula and the formula to: pageVars. PgVarRequestTemplate +" "+appVars.MessageText
  3. HTTP destination request:
    1. Destination: select the destination pointing to your AI deployment
    2. HTTP method: set to POST
    3. Request body type set to json
    4. Request body set to PgVarMessageToAi
    5. OPTIONAL INPUTS Path: set to
      /chat/completions?api-version=2024-10-21
      if you use ChatGPT4o like me. If you use different LLM, you have to use different path, suitable for your type and version of the LLM.
  4. Sep page variable PgVarSpinnerVisible to false
  5. Set page variable PgVarAiInterpretation to
    outputs["HTTP destination request"].resBodyParsed.choices[0].message.content
    - this will glow in red and there will be a message that it is incorrect, but it will work anyway.
  6. Set page variable PgVarInputTokens to
    outputs["HTTP destination request"].resBodyParsed.usage.prompt_tokens
    – again, ignore the red color and error message
  7. Set page variable PgVarOutputTokens to
    outputs["HTTP destination request"].resBodyParsed.usage.completion_tokens
    – again ignore the message
  8. Set page variable PgVarTotalTokens to
    outputs["HTTP destination request"].resBodyParsed.usage.total_tokens
    – the same story as above
  9. Alert: select formula for Dialog title and put
    "Failure status code: "+outputs["HTTP destination request"].failureStatusCode+" | Failure status text: "+outputs["HTTP destination request"].failureStatusText
    in the formula
  10. Alert: select formula for Dialog title and put
    "Error code: "+outputs["HTTP destination request"].error.code+" | Error message: "+outputs["HTTP destination request"].error.message
    in the formula

Save the application and test it in the preview – you should see interpretation by AI after a few seconds from pressing the “Send request to AI” button.

Build, deploy and enjoy! The demo application is ready. Now you can modify it up to your liking – adding features, beautifying the UI.

Please let me know if you would like to get the above project as .tar file, ready to upload to your SAP Build Apps environment.

entry.title: Setup your BTP Trial account and ABAP environment
entry.content:
Hi Community, 

Here are the steps to create your first BTP trial account and setup ABAP on cloud environment in eclipse. 

1. Create an account on SAP.com. If you already have S-user id or sap account, you can re-use that.   SAP Software Solutions | Business Applications and Technology

2.  Create BTP trial account

Go to SAP BTP cockpit  Instances and Subscriptions - trial < 5c30eb07trial < Trial Home - SAP BTP Cockpit

Login with your account

Choose US East(VA)-AWS region

 

Go to your Trial Account

Go to subaccounts

3. Create new instance and setup ABAP environment in BTP account

Subaccounts >> Instance and Subscriptions >> Create

Setup ABAP environment

Enter your BTP account e-mail id here

Create service key

Enter your e-mail id with which you created BTP account

4.

4. Download/Install and setup eclipse

SAP Development Tools

Launch eclipse from downloaded folder

Install ADT plug in

  1. In Eclipse, choose in the menu bar Help > Install New Software...
  2. Enter the URL https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/latest
  3. Press Enter to display the available features.
  4. Select ABAP Development Tools and choose Next.
  5. On the next wizard page, you get an overview of the features to be installed. Choose Next.
  6. Confirm the license agreements and choose Finish to start the installation.

Connect eclipse with ABAP on Cloud

File >> New >> ABAP Cloud Project

Go to your BTP account and click on view Service key

Copy url from your service key and paste in ABAP Service Instance URL in eclipse

Login with your BTP account in browser

Click Finish

Your ABAP environment on cloud will be connected.

 

Thanks for visiting. 

Please do like, share and comment.

Sangeeta Singh

entry.title: What is parameter MASS_PROCESSING of IDoc Inbound Function Modules?
entry.content:
Out of curiosity, what is the parameter MASS_PROCESSING of IDoc Inbound Function Modules?

Thanks.

Sandra

entry.title: Re: What is parameter MASS_PROCESSING of IDoc Inbound Fun...
entry.content:
How SAP fill the parameter:

  • If the IDoc(s) inbound processing is started via BD20/RBDAPP01, MASS_PROCESSING = 'X'.
  • If it's started via WE19, MASS_PROCESSING = ' '.
  • For other transaction codes and programs, I don't know.

How you can take advantage of this parameter:

  • I don't know.

entry.title: Introducing the RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench
entry.content:
Embarking on the RISE with SAP journey to transition to a private cloud environment can be overwhelming, but with the RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench, you have a powerful ally at your disposal to support you with the technical procedures and make them nearly seamless. Developed collaboratively with SAP Enterprise Cloud Services, the workbench is a dedicated tool ensuring a standardized and automated path to the private cloud, irrespective of your preferred migration approach.

What is the RISE with SAP system transition workbench?

The RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench is designed to be an extension of the family of software logistics tools such as SWPM and SUM in the context of private cloud transitions. It builds upon their capabilities to provide a highly automated guided procedure from the start to the end of the technical transition process.

 The RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench supports a growing portfolio of migration approaches, which can be used for all SAP NetWeaver-based on HANA target systems. Detailed documentation is available for each supported approach, providing clear guidelines and insights on how to proceed with your specific migration requirements. 

At the time of writing, the following approaches are available, either generally or as part of out early adopter program

    • SAP HANA Tenant Copy
    • SAP HANA Backup and Recovery
    • Homogeneous "Update and Copy"
    • System Conversion using SUM 2.0 (DMO with System Move)
    • System Conversion using SUM 2.0 (DMOVE2S4)

Each migration approach is facilitated through a structured guided procedure, referred to as "a cockpit". This cockpit is meticulously crafted to guide users through the technical procedure and is split into two main phases: the uptime phase and the downtime phase. This structure ensures that users have a clear understanding and control over the entire process.

 

New approaches and improvements are continuously bringing added, and you can track the same in our release note, and documentation.

What are the key benefits?

  • Extensive automated pre-requisite check report per migration approach, engineered to considerably reduce manual effort in verifying the system readiness for the selected approach and ensuring that all necessary criteria are met in a timely manner, reducing unplanned delays in the project runtime.
  • Automation integrated into the workbench. This automation is crafted to significantly minimize the time spent and the errors occurred in manual steps of the process, like system preparation, configuration, validation, etc. Reducing the amount of manual steps results in a stable, efficient, reliable and repeatable migration.
  • Reduced post-processing timelines by SAP ECS. The workbench ensures a consistent with SAP ECS’s standard and expectations, state of the system at the time of handover, which substantially decreases the need for time consuming follow-up actions and corrections.

Who is it for?

The RISE with SAP system transition workbench is available to all customers transitioning into the private cloud, supported by their respective migration teams, whether they are qualifying partners or SAP directly. Access to the workbench is straightforward, as it comes pre-installed on the migration server designated for the migration purpose. 

Staying Updated

Keeping the workbench up-to-date is easy. The latest version of the tool is maintained in the release note, and updates can be requested at any time through a service request in SAP for Me.

Your feedback matters

We value your input and are continuously working to improve the RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench. If you have any feedback or suggestions, please use the send feedback form in the tool or reach out to us directly.

Summary

The RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench is not just a tool - it's your navigation system in the digital transformation landscape. By radically reducing manual efforts, minimizing errors, and streamlining processes, it ensures that your journey to the private cloud is as smooth and efficient as possible. Check out our documentation and early adopter program and discover how you can leverage this incredible inovation to facilitate your migration endeavors.
